data.title: Asian markets mixed as BOJ holds rates and oil surge tests Wall Street’s record momentum
Ecuador signs $1.7B mining agreement with CMOC Group
data.description:
• Ecuador signed a mining contract with the local subsidiary ​of China's CMOC Group (CMCLF) (CMCLY) for the Los ‌Cangrejos mining project, Ecuador's energy ministry said on Monday, putting the total expected investment at more ​than $1.7B, according to reports.
  • The project, located in the El ​Oro province and developed by subsidiary ODIN ⁠Mining del Ecuador, is projected to ​generate $4.39 billion in total revenue for the state ​through taxes, royalties, and other fees, the ministry said in a statement.

data.title: Churchill Capital XII prices upsized $360M IPO at $10 per unit
data.description:
• Churchill Capital Corp XII (CXII) priced an upsized initial public offering at $360M, offering 36M units at $10.00 per unit. The units will be listed on the Nasdaq under the symbol “CXIIU” commencing today.
  • Each unit includes one Class A share + one-tenth of a warrant, with full warrants exercisable at $11.50 per share.
  • Churchill Capital Corp XII was founded by Michael Klein, who is also the founder and managing partner of M. Klein and Company, LLC. 
  • Shares and warrants are expected to trade separately as CXII and CXIIW after separation.
  • The SPAC has given the underwriters a 45-day option to purchase up to 5.4M additional units to cover over-allotment.
  • The company expects that the Class A ordinary shares and warrants will be listed on Nasdaq under the symbols “CXII” and “CXIIW,” respectively.
  • The offering is expected to close on April 29, 2026.

data.title: BYD draws EU scrutiny over labor abuse allegations at Hungary factory: report
data.description:
Electric car giant BYD (BYDDF) (BYDDY) has become the first Chinese business to be raised in the European Parliament over allegations of labor abuses in Hungary, CNBC has learned, following a watchdog’s investigation into working conditions at the site.

Contractors hired to build BYD’s factory in Hungary allegedly kept thousands of employees working seven days a week, with shifts lasting more than 12 hours a day, according to a report published on April 14 by New York-based watchdog China Labor Watch. The group said it interviewed 50 workers and visited the factory site three times since October 2025.

China Labor Watch, a U.S.-based nonprofit organization that has tracked worker conditions since its founding in 2000, shared the report’s findings with EU government representatives. Earlier this month, three members of the European Parliament formally asked the European Commission about the alleged labor abuses in Hungary, CNBC reported.

The allegations by China Labor Watch mark the first time claims of labor abuses linked to a Chinese-owned auto business manufacturing in the European Union have been brought to the attention of the European Commission, according to checks by CNBC.

In February, a worker reportedly died on-site during a crane operation. Citing conversations with workers, CLW founder Qiang Li told CNBC there had been more deaths on site.

data.title: Chinese EV battery maker CATL falls 8% after $5B fundraising plan
data.description:
• Contemporary Amperex Technology, or CATL (CTATF) (CYATY) shares slipped 8.5% Tuesday after the EV battery giant unveiled plans for a roughly $5B equity offering in Hong Kong.
  • CATL, which makes lithium-ion batteries for electric vehicles, is seeking to raise $39.2B Hong Kong dollars (about $5B) through a private placement, as it accelerates investment in its renewable energy business against the backdrop of a global oil crunch.

data.title: BOJ holds rates at 0.75% as Middle East conflict fuels 2.8% inflation forecast
data.description:
The Bank of Japan maintained its short-term policy rate at 0.75% during its April 2026 meeting, though the decision saw a notable shift in board sentiment.

In a 6–3 vote, dissenters Hajime Takata, Naoki Tamura, and Junko Nakagawa broke from the majority to propose an immediate hike to 1.0%, citing upside risks to inflation from the Iran conflict.

Reflecting these pressures, the BOJ raised its FY2026 core inflation outlook to 2.8% (from 1.9%) and downgraded its growth forecast to 0.5%, as rising energy prices begin to weigh on the moderate recovery.

At the same time, policymakers said the FY2025 GDP projection was slightly lifted to 1.0% from 0.9%, lifted by last year’s trade deal with Washington.

data.title: Polaris beats Q1 estimates, reaffirms guidance
data.description:
• Polaris press release (PII): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.13 beats by $0.53.
  • Revenue of $1.66B (+7.8% Y/Y) beats by $20M.
  • First quarter reported diluted loss per share was $0.83• Primary factors affecting first quarter sales were higher shipments coupled with positive price and lowerpromotions.• First quarter market share gains in off-road vehicles (ORV) and snowmobiles.• Total Polaris retail sales for the quarter, excluding Youth, were up one percent versus last year. ORV retail saleswere up three percent, excluding Youth.• Polaris reaffirmed full year 2026 guidance provided on March 3, 2026.
  • The Company is reaffirming 2026 full year adjusted sales and adjusted EPS guidance. The Company expects 2026adjusted sales to be between $7.15 billion to $7.30 billion (vs. consensus of $7.29B) and adjusted EPS of $1.60 to $1.70 (vs. consensus of $1.67)
